name: CQC
description: Gears often carry small weapons like daggers as a last-ditch measure. Others mount small blades and drills into the frame to give your unit something to use when all other options are out. Even those that cannot punch or kick can still ram themselves into the enemy as a last resort.
effect: This Weapon suffers an innate Disadvantage to its use, but cannot be disabled through Maiming.

name: Vulcans
description: Most giant robots these days come with anti-personnel guns integrated into the frame, usually going on the arms at 20mm caliber or the head at 10mm caliber. Even those who don't can improvise by picking up vehicles or even buildings to toss. The collateral damage won't make you any friends, though.
effect: This Weapon suffers an innate Disadvantage to its use, but cannot be disabled through Maiming.

name: Arm Guardian
description: A giant shoulder guard with pointy ends might not sound like much. It is pretty useful for pointmen trying to draw the heat away from the rest of their team, though.
effect: Passing the Might Test with this Weapon increases your Defense by 3 against any Might Tests from the Enemy you just attacked until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Assassin Blade
description: This set of two small blades is awful for a straight up one on one match but perfect against exposed backs.
effect: This Weapon will not attack your Allies in a Duel and gains an Advantage against Enemies that are in a Duel with someone else.

name: Boosted Lance
description: This rocket-powered lance with a detachable, explosive tip is a favorite of high-speed shock troops. Ideal for hit and run tactics and an enabler of pretend-jousting.
effect: Passing the Might Test with this Weapon while charging in a straight line deals that Enemy an additional amount of Damage equal to half the amount of Zones you Moved during the same Offensive Action. You may keep going in the same direction if you have any remaining Zones of movement left and it adds to the Damage dealt. If you end the charge in the same Zone as the Enemy you may Engage them. Boosted Lance cannot be used to escape from a Duel.

name: Countersword
description: A weapon made for quick-draw techniques. The blade and its sheath are designed for a faster drawing speed that conserves momentum and slays within a single stroke. These blades are terribly inaccurate otherwise and break often when used to parry, so don't do that.
effect: Technique. Choosing to use this Weapon skips your next Action this Turn. The next time an Enemy within your Movement range would take an Offensive Action against you before your next Turn, you can Move up to and attack them. Countersword has an additional Advantage to its Might Test and interrupts the Enemy's own attack, making it possible to destroy or disable them before they hit you. If no Enemies trigger the counterattack, Countersword is not spent.

name: Chainblade
description: Giant chainsaws are clumsy and jam in the middle of the action way too often. The rest of the time they are terrifying and devastating. A favorite of operators who think themselves lucky or simply have no idea what they're doing.
effect: Unreliable. Passing the Might Test with this Weapon deals that Enemy an additional amount of Damage equal to the current Tension.

name: Dueling Blade
description: While not as generally useful as other melee equipment, fencing weapons remain a formidable threat in the hands of those that know how to keep their enemies pinned.
effect: If at the beginning of your Turn you are in a Duel with an Enemy, Dueling Blade gains an Advantage against that Enemy for this Turn.

name: Finger Net
description: A giant net made to restrain and capture Mechabeasts for research. By installing them in a Gear's fingertips they can be shot as a surprise in the middle of close quarters combat, making sure they will snare their prey.
effect: Long Range, Blast (1), One Shot. All Units within the Weapon's area of effect have their Guard and Speed halved until the end of your next Turn.

name: Great Crusher
description: A giant hammer filled with explosives that self destructs on impact. There are certainly smarter weapons out there, but there aren't many with its sheer brutality.
effect: Blast (1), Crippling, Long Range, One Shot.

name: Hook Launcher
description: A nasty piece of work used to single out key targets and disrupt enemy formations. Its range is not the best and reloading it is a pain, but when it works it just ruins a whole enemy squadron's battle plan.
effect: Long Range, Crippling, One Shot. When you pass the Might Test with this Weapon you may have the target dragged all the way to your own Zone and force them into a Duel with you.

name: Jackhammer Stake
description: Bayonets haven't lost their use in this day and age. They're even better when you use them to repeatedly pummel a foe with firepower as they're stuck to it.
effect: You may choose to spend this Weapon when using it, as though it were a One Shot. If you do, it gains two Advantages to its Might Test. Effects that restore the function of One Shot Weapons can be used to grant additional uses of this ability.

name: Kamaitachi
description: A sword that releases shockwaves ahead with every swing. Complaints that research on these was a waste of funding when we already have guns were dismissed. Those poor souls lacked the necessary warrior's spirit to understand.
effect: Line, Long Range.

name: Magnet Rod
description: This oversized bat is attuned to home in on G-Reactors. Assuming there is enough of a difference between the strength of the G-Reactors between duelists, this weapon has perfect accuracy.
effect: This Weapon gains an additional Advantage to its Might Test when used against Enemies with an Energy Attribute higher than yours.

name: Pressure Point Attack
description: Martial artists know how and where to hit in order to kill any human with a single blow. It is much harder to do for giant robots and even tougher on giant monsters, though, so they have to settle with maiming them instead.
effect: Crippling, One Shot. Passing the Might Test with this Weapon will destroy that Enemy's current Level of Threshold. This happens after the Weapon deals its regular Damage. The additional Damage is fixed and cannot be reduced or prevented in any way.

name: Rocket Punch
description: The Rocket Punch is a staple for those that like keeping to their fists even at range, even if it is understandably harder to aim than shooting a proper gun.
effect: Long Range, Slow. This Weapon benefits from an additional Advantage to its Might Test and its maximum Range is your Systems plus three instead of your Systems plus one.

name: Rocket Sword
description: From the R&D team that brought you the Boosted Lance, we present the Rocket Sword. This blade cleaves its way through multiple enemies on the way to its destination and takes you along for the ride.
effect: One Shot. Charge in a straight line when using this Weapon. Rocket Sword deals an amount of Damage equal to the current Tension to all Enemies that come into Range during the charge. This happens even if the attack otherwise misses and includes the target. If you end the charge in the same Zone as the Enemy you may Engage them.

name: Shocking Swordwhip
description: A flexible, upscaled version of the technology employed in the Stun Rod. The most obvious advantage of these is their extended reach, but the flexibility of the weapon can be used to ensnare and hold Mechabeasts.
effect: Crippling, Long Range, Unreliable. This Weapon inflicts an additional Disadvantage on passing the Might Test.

name: Stumbling Fists Style
description: You confuse your enemy with awkward swaying and staggering to lure them into a false sense of security, then use their own strength against them. Watching a giant tentacle monster smack itself is always fun.
effect: Slow, Unreliable. Increase the result of your Might Tests when using this Weapon by an amount equal to how much the target's Might Attribute is greater than yours.

name: Stun Rod
description: Employed by Mechabeast capture teams to subdue them rather than to kill them. Multiple units equipped with Stun Rods can render the fearsome giant monsters largely ineffectual.
effect: This Weapon inflicts an additional Disadvantage when using the Suppress Action.

name: Whirlwind Attack
description: A favorite of close combat specialists for crowd control. Spinning in place can look rather silly when it fails to accomplish much, but it quickly makes up for it with its effective lethality in the hands of an experienced ace.
effect: Burst, Slow, Unreliable. This Weapon benefits from an additional Advantage to its Might Test.

name: Zweihander
description: Sheer size is the name of the game with some weapons. From giant tomahawks to enormous spears, there is a lot of variety out there for Gear pilots who want to just destroy whatever is on their way without having to resort to energy-based equipment. The downside is that they are unwieldy and often leave you open to counterattacks.
effect: Technique. Enemies attacked with this Weapon gain two Advantages to Might Tests against you until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Anti-Air Missiles
description: Air dominance is still very much a factor even in this new era of Gears. Most defensive lines have emplacements with these, ready to shoot down planes, anti-gravity Gears and swarms of flying Mechabeasts.
effect: This Weapon gains two Advantages when used against targets with Flyer or using Anti-Gravity.

name: Anti-Mecha Cannon
description: A giant single-shot pistol with an armor-piercing round that explodes within the target. Needs a dedicated reloading assistant for maximum efficiency.
effect: Crippling, Long Range, One Shot.

name: Assault Rifle
description: The standard issue in today's day and age, useful in all situations thanks to its solid accuracy and reliable range.
effect: Long Range. You choose Areas Maimed and will not attack your Allies in a Duel when using this Weapon.

name: Bombardment
description: For those times when you have to pull out all the stops, just point your massive armaments at the center of the enemy forces and watch everything go up in flames.
effect: Blast (1), One Shot. This Weapon deals an additional amount of Damage equal to the current Tension to everything inside its area of effect. This happens even if the attack otherwise misses.

name: Hyper Launcher
description: A simple launcher for rocket propelled explosives. Difficult to aim yet brutally effective enough to compensate.
effect: Blast (3), Unreliable.

name: Electro-Sapper Pods
description: You launch miniaturized combat drones that will attach to a target and proceed to interfere with their internal systems. These drones don't last very long, but in their short lifespan they will cause lasting damage.
effect: One Shot. Passing the Might Test with this Weapon will deal that Enemy an additional amount of Damage equal to your Systems.

name: Lockdown Missile
description: A series of split missiles that spread many gravity manipulators around a target area to trap those within in a massive short-lived but extremely powerful forcefield.
effect: Blast (1), One Shot. This Weapon turns the outside border of its area of effect into Impassable Terrain until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Macrowave Cannon
description: The Macrowave Cannon absorbs ambient electromagnetic energy and releases it while scattering a multitude of pellets. The last thing that the poor souls in the way of the blast will hear is a loud 'DING'. It gets less funny when users notice the recoil will shock even the sturdiest of Gears and daze the most resilient of pilots.
effect: Line, Long Range, Technique. This Weapon requires one Action be spent Aiming with it and its successful use will automatically make you lose your next Action afterwards. Macrowave Cannon has an additional Advantage to its Might Test and its Line effect is 3 Zones wide, with the target in the middle line.

name: Missile Massacre
description: A figurative circus display of missiles shot in a pattern that makes trying to evade them nearly futile. This advanced maneuver impacts from multiple angles at once to increase the chances of a direct hit. But most pilots will learn your patterns from this move, and will have a much easier time avoiding you afterwards.
effect: Technique. After attacking an Enemy with this Weapon, you suffer a Disadvantage to all further Might Tests against that Enemy for the rest of the Operation.

name: Mounted Blaster
description: These shoulder-mounted guns are too powerful to use without bracing. They make up for it with accuracy and armor-piercing power, but the rounds are too large for long-distance bombardment.
effect: This Weapon benefits from an additional Advantage to its Might Test but you may not Move as part of your Actions when using it. If you're unable to Move for whatever reason, you cannot use it.

name: Rail Bazooka
description: Designed to make big explosions at a distance, it is not exactly a sniper's dream weapon but it is very fun to use. The auto-loading mechanism is a piece of work though, and it takes its sweet time getting ready.
effect: Long Range, Blast (2), Slow.

name: Radiation Bomb
description: One of the worst payloads you could let anyone take with themselves to the battlefield. Radiation Bombs are one step below nuclear weaponry and are only allowed in extreme circumstances.
effect: Blast (1), One Shot. This Weapon turns all Zones affected into Extreme Terrain until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Rapidfire Machinecannon
description: This gun walks the thin line between expensive gimmick and absolute genius. It has only one thing going for it: A ridiculous rate of fire and the magazine size to sustain it.
effect: This Weapon inflicts an additional Disadvantage when using the Suppress Action.

name: Resonance Cannon
description: This cannon fires sonic bursts that shatter enemies at the structural level. It is unwieldy and uses up all of its ammunition with each shot, but a mere graze can be as damaging as a direct hit.
effect: Slow, Unreliable. Passing the Might Test with this Weapon will destroy that Enemy's current Level of Threshold. This happens after the Weapon deals its regular Damage. The additional Damage is fixed and cannot be reduced or prevented in any way.

name: Riot Weapon
description: Sometimes you just want to blow holes in everything nearby. Claymore launchers or an upsized shotgun will do that job just fine. Just point it away from your friends.
effect: Line.

name: Sentry Turret
description: The Sentry Turret is a tripodal device, more like a portable artillery platform than an attack drone. As a stationary attack drone, it has better accuracy than most. Unfortunately, it is useless without a controller pointing it in the right direction at all times.
effect: Long Range, Remote. This Remote has a Guard of 5 and a Speed of 0 plus two innate Advantages to its Might Test.

name: Sniper Rifle
description: The weapon of choice for the mecha-scale sharpshooter. The only issue is that it performs terribly when unscoped, which is to say, it does not perform at all.
effect: Long Range. This Weapon cannot be fired without first taking the Aim Action with it, but gains a third Advantage from doing so.

name: Superheavy Machinegun
description: An oversized firearm that lends itself to accusations about trying to overcompensate for something. The recoil is terrible, forcing the Gear to brace itself and remain immobile. Even then most of the shots go in the wrong direction anyway, but after all is said and done there is no better gun for providing suppressive fire.
effect: Line, Long Range, Unreliable. This Weapon can be used to Suppress despite having the Line ability and inflicts an additional Disadvantage when doing so. You can't Move when using this Weapon. If you're unable to Move for whatever reason, you cannot use it either.

name: Torpedoes
description: Torpedoes have better range and are cheaper to produce than beam weaponry. Coupled with Anti-Air Missiles, they will be the preferred weapon for battle at sea.
effect: Long Range. This Weapon has two innate Advantages to its Might Test, but may only be used while in water and against targets that are also in water.

name: Tracer Rifle
description: The next step in tracer ammunition technology is to outright build individual computers into their bullets. Enemies hit by one of these will feed invaluable aiming data to your allies.
effect: When you pass the Might Test with this Weapon, Allies who also attack the same Enemy gain an Advantage to their own Might Tests until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Beam Saber
description: A favorite for its low energy consumption, solid armor-piercing power, compact size and overall practicality. Mass produced and made standard issue for both factions.
effect: Beam (Boost 1).

name: Beam Ripper
description: Giant beam weapons taking the shape of axes, scythes or other similar blades. They are unwieldy devices used for chopping the key body parts of giant regenerating monsters to assist in subduing and capturing them alive.
effect: Beam (Boost 2). When you Boost this Weapon it gains the Crippling and Slow abilities.

name: Burnout Edge
description: A weaponized projector of short-lived waves that disrupt the flow of Gravagnium. Just point the weapon in this 'Burnout' mode at things and watch their systems fail.
effect: Beam (Boost 2). When you Boost and pass the Might Test with this Weapon, you halve the target's available Energy.

name: Magneburst
description: An offensive use of forcefield technology, releasing gravitational waves around the user. This weapon is harmful to the environment and is thus usually shunned.
effect: Beam (Boost 2). When you Boost this Weapon it gains the Burst ability and increases your Defense by 2 until the beginning of your next Turn.

name: Extending Blade
description: This weapon's length is theoretically infinite. The only reason it doesn't go farther than it currently does is because they just haven't figured out how to keep the excessive output from breaking the device itself.
effect: Beam (Boost 3). When you Boost this Weapon its Maximum Range becomes 10.

name: Fangs
description: Fangs are drones with miniaturized beam sabers made for swarming targets. They have miniaturized Overboosters for chasing targets and are maneuverable enough to take advantage of their small size, making them a pain to hit.
effect: Beam (Boost 3), Remote. This Remote has a Guard of 10 and a Speed of 5.

name: Beam Rifle
description: Cheap to produce and easy to use, it makes a great all-purpose weapon if you can get around its craving for energy.
effect: Beam (Boost 1).

name: Graviton Gun
description: A gun that can lift enemies off the ground. It does not have that much controlling power, but it is still very useful to assist with a clutch shot.
effect: Beam (Boost 2), Line. When you Boost and pass the Might Test with this Weapon, you may slide the target 1 Zone in a direction of your choice along the Weapon's area of effect. Should it be sent in the direction of another Enemy whose Defense you've also beat, they both crash and suffer 1 point of Damage.

name: Point Singularity Projector
description: A variant of the Graviton Gun, this Point Singularity Projector creates pockets of increased gravity, holding targets in place momentarily or slowing them down.
effect: Beam (Boost 2). When you Boost and use the Assist Action with this Weapon you may choose to either gain an Advantage to your Might Test or to grant an Advantage to two allies instead of one. You gain the chosen Boost plus the Long Range and Slow abilities.

name: Powered Rifle
description: A weapon for providing support fire at long distances, combining accuracy with penetration power. It can pierce through all but the most heavily armored of foes if you take a moment to align the sights.
effect: Beam (Boost 2), Long Range. When you Boost this Weapon its Maximum Range increases by 5. Powered Rifle can target only Enemies that are at Range 5 or higher.

name: Bits
description: The most common system of remote weapons. Bits pack a mean punch for their size and are fairly maneuverable.
effect: Beam (Boost 3), Crippling, Remote, Slow. This Remote has a Guard of 5 and a Speed of 5. When you Boost this Weapon it gains the Long Range ability.

name: Charge Cannon
description: A pistol-shaped beam cannon with two firing modes. Continuous fire mode provides a consistent supply of gunfire. Charged mode releases an explosive shot that can hit multiple targets but forces the weapon to go on cooldown.
effect: Beam (Boost 3). When you Boost this Weapon it gains an additional Advantage to the Might Test plus the Blast (2) and Slow abilities.

name: Gravity Knuckle
description: An equippable giant knuckle that repulses objects with tremendous force on activation.
effect: Beam (Boost 4). When you Boost and pass the Might Test with this Weapon, you may push the target up to 3 Zones away from you in a direction of your choice. If it collides with another Enemy, the movement stops. If you've also beaten the second Enemy's Defense they both take 3 Damage.

name: Incinerator
description: Someone just had to devise the equivalent of a giant flame thrower. The Grid shuns this weapon, but the Technocracy loves it for its unparalleled potential to terrorize enemies and urban warfare crowd control potential.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Line, Long Range, Overheating. When you Boost Incinerator, all Zones affected become Extreme Terrain until the beginning of your next Turn. You can spare your own Zone from its effects.

name: Ground Zero
description: The uninhibited version of the Magneburst. You expel pure, raw Element G in a highly dangerous explosion with you at its center. Handle with care.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Burst, Technique, Overheating. This Weapon turns the Zone you are standing on into Extreme Terrain. When you Boost Ground Zero it also turns the other Zones it affects into Extreme Terrain. (Extreme Terrain created by Ground Zero doesn't fade with time).

name: Radiant Fist
description: The ultimate in close range finishers. The unit is equipped with an extremely damaging radiation pulse that makes its hands glow with an awesome power.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Technique, Overheating. When you Boost this Weapon it gains a second Advantage to its Might Test from doing so.

name: Double Blaster
description: A double barreled beam weapon that can be split into two separate rifles, making it as good as two guns in one. It is not just versatile but powerful, perhaps too powerful for its own stability, tending to overload and self destruct after consecutive uses.
effect: Beam (Boost 4). When you Boost this Weapon you may choose to have either a second Advantage to the Might Test or to attack two Enemies instead of one. You gain the chosen Boost plus the Overheating ability.

name: Lux Cannon
description: A giant laser cannon that pierces through most barriers and is almost impossible to dodge. It consumes a lot of energy and has a lengthy recharging period.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Long Range, Slow. When you Boost this Weapon it ignores the effects of Active Defenses.

name: Oblivion Buster
description: This dramatically named giant cannon creates what can only be described as a miniature black hole at a target point and is the epitome of weaponized gravity technology. A direct hit means the target will implode spectacularly and a graze will still tear away one or more limbs caught in the vortex. Be careful with it, the Oblivion Buster has a tendency to malfunction and make parts of its user vanish into the aether.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Long Range, Technique, Overheating. You may not Move as part of your Actions when using this Weapon and if you're unable to Move for whatever reason, you cannot use it. When you Boost Oblivion Buster and pass the Might Test with it, it will destroy that Enemy's current Level of Threshold (after dealing its regular Damage) but you do not regenerate Energy during your next Turn. The additional Damage is fixed and cannot be reduced or prevented in any way.

name: Reactor Overdrive
description: You overload and expose the Gear's generators redirecting the power surge outside. The brutal energy blast might cause lasting damage to your machine, but is much more certain to do even worse for whoever is on the other side of the scope.
effect: Beam (Boost 4), Technique, Overheating. When Boosted this Weapon gains the Line and Long Range abilities.
